title: segmentation_proposal_{source_document_id}.json -- bundle document segmentation proposal v0.1
description: A proposal for how one raw bundle PDF should be split into logical documents, produced by tools/segment_case.py BEFORE any OCR runs. Stage 1's output is document structure, not text. One file per bundle document (segmentation_proposal_DOC_XXX.json), never a shared flat filename -- the same silent-overwrite bug that hit ocr_result/classification_result/redaction_result would hit a case with two bundles. This is shared review state, not a component output, so it does not use the common output envelope. Written/read only through the DAO. A proposal starts review_status: pending and only a human moves it to approved; tools/segment_case.py split refuses to run until then, and refuses again if any individual segment is still pending.

source_document_id.description: owner: segmentation. The bundle being segmented -- its existing document_manifest.json entry.

source_file_path.description: The bundle under data/raw/. Never a source-cases/ path: source-cases/ is immutable and is not read by this stage.

review_status.description: Case-level gate. Starts pending. Only a human sets approved/rejected. Approving this is NOT enough to split -- every segment must also be approved or edited.

segments.description: May be empty: a manual-mode skeleton has no segments until a human fills them, and a failed parse deliberately produces zero segments rather than inventing boundaries.

unassigned_pages.description: Pages covered by no segment, recorded explicitly rather than left for a reviewer to compute. Non-empty is a normal intermediate state (manual mode starts with every page here); it is only an error at split time, which refuses to run while any page is unassigned.

warnings.description: Non-fatal problems a human should see before approving: parse failures, fallback saturation, blank or sideways pages.

method.description: How this proposal was produced. Recorded in enough detail that a token-cost or quality regression is diagnosable from the artifact alone, without re-running anything.

ocr_performed.description: Structurally const, not merely false. Stage 1 does not transcribe text, and a proposal claiming otherwise is rejected by schema validation rather than by convention -- the cheapest available enforcement of the stage boundary.

mode.description: manual = a human read the contact sheets and entered ranges; no model was called. vision_proposal = a model proposed boundaries. Deliberately NOT one value per backend: claude-cli and local-vlm are both vision_proposal, distinguished by provider_name/model_name, so a provider swap does not change the mode's meaning.

render_dpi.description: Used for the full-page fallback path. Contact-sheet cells are rendered directly at cell size via a zoom matrix, not at a DPI, because the vision long-edge cap makes any higher render resolution pure waste.

crop_ratio.description: Fraction of each page's height kept, measured from the top. Document titles and form headers sit in the top fifth on this corpus; the default 0.33 keeps that with margin.

sheet_pixel_budget.description: The achieved sheet geometry, not the cap. Recorded because grid/crop choices interact with the vision API's downscaling in a way that is invisible after the fact otherwise.

contact_sheets.description: Paths point into gitignored scratch and will not survive a clean checkout -- recorded anyway because during review a human needs the exact sheet the model saw.

pages.description: Pages the model could not judge from the cropped cell.

saturated.description: True when more pages needed full-page review than the cap allows. The fallback is then SKIPPED entirely rather than run: saturation means the crop ratio or grid is wrong for this bundle, which is a tuning signal for a human, not something to spend a large number of extra vision calls papering over.

segment.description: One proposed logical document: a contiguous page range within the bundle.

page_start.description: 1-based, inclusive -- matching intake_case's --split spec and run_checkpoint1's --page-start.

page_end.description: 1-based, inclusive. page_end >= page_start is enforced by segment_case.validate_segments(), not here: JSON Schema cannot compare sibling values, and faking it with allOf would be worse than an honest tool-side check.

provisional_document_type.description: owner: segmentation. A PRE-OCR VISUAL GUESS from a cropped, downscaled contact-sheet cell. Not authoritative and must not be copied into document_manifest.json's document_type. Unlike pre_flagged_type -- which a human asserted and classification therefore trusts -- this is model inference from a deliberately low-fidelity image, so document-pipeline's checkpoint 1 still runs its own classification against the real OCR'd text.

provisional_type_label.description: The model's own words for the document type (e.g. 후유장해진단서), unconstrained by the closed document_type enum. Kept because that enum has 8 buckets for a corpus with far more real form types: forcing 후유장해진단서 and a routine outpatient 진단서 both into diagnosis_certificate discards exactly the distinction a human reviewer needs to check the boundary.

confidence.description: null in manual mode -- a human entering a boundary they read directly is not expressing model confidence.

boundary_evidence.description: What was visible at page_start that marks a new document (title block, letterhead, page-1-of-N reset). This is what makes human review possible in bounded time: without it a reviewer must re-derive every boundary from the sheets themselves.

review_status.description: edited = a human changed the proposed range and approves the changed one. Distinct from approved so the evaluation record shows where the model was corrected.

needs_full_page.description: The cropped cell was insufficient and this segment's boundary came from (or still needs) a full-page look.

orientation_suspect.description: The page's content bounding box is wider than tall, suggesting a sideways scan. page.rotation is 0 on these -- it is content orientation, not a PDF flag -- so a top crop captures a table's left edge rather than a title, and the cell cannot be judged.

assigned_document_id.description: null until split runs. Set to the newly created DOC_XXX so the proposal remains a usable audit record afterward.
